title: Kimi K3 Achieves Frontier AI Parity via Distillation
summary: A Chinese lab has achieved performance parity with frontier AI models like GPT-5.6 and Opus 4.8 by distilling knowledge from larger models, releasing the Kimi K3 model. This milestone shows that model distillation can rapidly close the gap with leading AI labs, potentially disrupting the competitive landscape and raising national security concerns about open-weight models. Kimi K3 has 2.8 trillion parameters and is priced at $3/$15 per million tokens input/output, comparable to GPT-5.6 Sol \($5/$30\) and Opus 4.8 \($5/$25\). Some users report that it takes significantly longer on complex tasks compared to leading models.
context: Model distillation transfers knowledge from a large &\#x27;teacher&\#x27; model to a smaller &\#x27;student&\#x27; model, enabling efficient performance at lower cost. Open-weight models provide public access to model parameters, unlike closed-source frontier models. This technique has been widely discussed in the AI community as a way to democratize AI capabilities.
discussion: Commenters are divided: some view distillation as an inevitable and legitimate method, while others worry about national security crackdowns. User SwellJoe reports that Kimi K3 struggled with a task that OpenAI&\#x27;s model handled quickly, indicating possible quality trade-offs. User credit\_guy notes the parameter and pricing parity, suggesting direct competition.

references.title: What are Open Source and Open Weight Models ? | Analytics Vidhya
